classification_rationale: A thin TypeScript scaffolding package (README.md:11 "intentionally thin and lightweight... NOT production logic"; package.json:3 "Plugin registry and template pack for AFI Protocol"). It defines local plugin types and a hardcoded in-memory registry of stub plugins (signal/analyzer/scorer/validator/executor/observer), all explicitly STUB-only returning empty/zero results (.afi-codex.json:73 "stubImplementationsOnly": true). It is the reference extension surface for the reference spine (afi-reactor DAG + afi-core ElizaOS runtime), not a normative protocol artifact: it carries no canonical schemas, no contracts, no determinism/replay rules, and its PluginManifest interface is a local TS type unconnected to the normative plugin-manifest.schema.json in afi-config. Classified REFERENCE_IMPL rather than SUPPORTING because it ships executable runtime extension code wired to reactor/core/eliza, and rather than NORMATIVE because nothing here is an interop rule other repos must validate against.
primary_purpose: Central plugin registry and template/stub pack that defines the extension surface (types, manifests, and placeholder implementations for signal generators, analyzers, scorers, validators, executors, observers) for the AFI reference stack (afi-reactor DAG orchestrator and afi-core ElizaOS runtime), with all real logic deferred to runtime/infra repos.

reference_only_assumptions.quote: It serves as the canonical extension surface for: - afi-reactor (DAG orchestrator) - afi-core (ElizaOS runtime layer)
the canonical extension surface for afi-reactor (DAG orchestrator) and afi-core (ElizaOS runtime layer).
NOTE: Integrates with T.S.S.D. Vault (afi-infra)... notes: "Integrates with T.S.S.D. Vault for historical signal replay."
NOTE: Full signal types live in afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts
afi-plugins MUST NOT modify DAG structure or orchestration logic. Dependency direction: afi-reactor -> afi-plugins (reactor consumes plugins)

contradictions.title: Reactor/core/eliza hardcoded as canonical runtime targets (reactor-as-THE-orchestrator tension)
contradictions.detail: The plugin type system enumerates a CLOSED set of runtime targets (afi-reactor, afi-core, eliza-tool, hybrid) and brands the repo the 'canonical extension surface' for afi-reactor + afi-core. A portable protocol should let plugins target any conformant DAG/runtime; the closed enum and 'canonical' language bake the reference spine into the type contract, so a third-party DAG framework cannot be a first-class runtimeTarget without editing this repo.
contradictions.evidence: src/types/plugin.ts:22-26 'export type RuntimeTarget = | "afi-reactor" | "afi-core" | "eliza-tool" | "hybrid";' ; README.md:7 'canonical extension surface'

contradictions.title: Validator replay coupled to a specific store (T.S.S.D. Vault / afi-infra) rather than the abstract evidence plane
contradictions.detail: The replay validator template names T.S.S.D. Vault in afi-infra as THE backing store for historical replay, and points to afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts as the source of 'full signal types'. Per the North Star the evidence/vault plane is pluggable (Mongo/PostgreSQL/Timescale/Influx) behind vault.schema.json; tying replay to one org-specific vault implementation narrows the portable evidence boundary.
contradictions.evidence: src/templates/validator/halfDecayReplayTemplate.ts:45 'NOTE: Integrates with T.S.S.D. Vault (afi-infra).' ; :95 'STUB: Real logic integrates with T.S.S.D. Vault in afi-infra' ; blofinTrendPullbackTemplate.ts:22 'Full signal types live in afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts'

contradictions.title: Local PluginManifest type is disconnected from the normative plugin-manifest.schema.json
contradictions.detail: The North Star lists plugin-manifest.schema.json as a NORMATIVE artifact. This repo defines its own PluginManifest TS interface and uses string-only configSchemaRef/inputSchemaRef values (e.g. 'BlofinTrendPullbackConfig') that resolve to nothing here. No validation against afi-config schemas exists, and AGENTS.md even references a planned (non-existent) 'npm run validate:plugins'. So the registry is not provably conformant to the canonical manifest schema.
contradictions.evidence: src/types/plugin.ts:45-81 'export interface PluginManifest { ... configSchemaRef?: string; ... }' ; AGENTS.md:35-37 'npm run validate:plugins (planned command - may be stubbed in this phase)'

contradictions.title: Determinism / pinned-version / replay-reproducibility rules absent from validator and scorer surface
contradictions.detail: Scorer and validator are the plugin kinds most relevant to deterministic scoring and challenge-ready replay, yet neither template nor its types capture pinned plugin/analyst/validator versions, topology pinning, or snapshotted external-data semantics. Replay is treated as a 'mode' string and a half-decay heuristic only. This is an omission against decentralization rules 2-3 (pinned transforms; same inputs+rules => same outputs).
contradictions.evidence: src/types/context.ts:108 'mode?: "pre-mint" | "post-mint" | "replay";' ; scorer/README.md:32 'Composite score (weighted average: a*PoI + b*PoInsight)' (no pinning/determinism contract)

upstream: afi-infra (T.S.S.D. Vault client, tssd/types.ts, exchange clients)
afi-math (referenced for statistical scoring - scorer README)
downstream: afi-reactor (consumes plugin defs as DAG nodes)

notable_findings.title: Entire repo is STUB-only scaffolding; no production/protocol logic present
notable_findings.evidence: .afi-codex.json:70-75 '"phase1Constraints": { "noNetworkCalls": true, "noBlockchainSDKs": true, "stubImplementationsOnly": true ... }' ; README.md:58 'All implementations are STUBS ONLY in Phase 1.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as reference extension scaffolding; do not cite any code here as protocol law. When wired to real runtimes, validate manifests against afi-config plugin-manifest.schema.json.

notable_findings.title: TSSD/Vault referenced only as a downstream afi-infra dependency, never implemented
notable_findings.evidence: src/templates/validator/halfDecayReplayTemplate.ts:5 ' - T.S.S.D. Vault integration for historical signals' ; :45 'NOTE: Integrates with T.S.S.D. Vault (afi-infra).'
notable_findings.recommendation: Clarify in template comments that the vault is a pluggable evidence-plane interface (vault.schema.json), not specifically the afi-infra T.S.S.D. implementation.

notable_findings.title: No USS/CPJ ingest dialect handling anywhere
notable_findings.evidence: grep for 'usignal|USS|cpj' across src/docs returned zero hits; AFIRawSignal is a local minimal shape (blofinTrendPullbackTemplate.ts:25-32) not the canonical USS v1.1 record.
notable_findings.recommendation: When signal-generator plugins emit real signals, conform output to USS v1.1 (afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1) rather than the ad-hoc AFIRawSignal placeholder.

notable_findings.title: No mint/receipt/emissions surface; epoch present only as optional context
notable_findings.evidence: src/types/context.ts:82-83 'Epoch ID for batch processing / epochId?: string;' ; grep for 'mintForSignal|coordinateMint|beneficiary|AFIMintCoordinator|EmissionsMinted' returned zero hits. AGENTS.md:128 forbids tokenomics here.
notable_findings.recommendation: None - correctly out of scope for commitment plane; keep mint/emissions in afi-token/afi-mint.

notable_findings.title: No analytics-plane coupling (Kafka/warehouse/snowflake) - correctly absent
notable_findings.evidence: grep for 'kafka|warehouse|snowflake|datalake' across src/docs returned zero hits. MongoDB TSSD is the implemented evidence store for the reference spine.

notable_findings.title: No multi-engine vault DB coupling (Postgres/Timescale/Influx/Mongo) in code
notable_findings.evidence: grep for 'postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb|mongodb' across src/docs returned zero hits; only the abstract 'T.S.S.D. Vault' label appears.
notable_findings.recommendation: None - no DB tunnel vision in this repo; vault is referenced abstractly.

notable_findings.title: Reactor labeled 'DAG orchestrator' and afi-plugins 'canonical extension surface' across docs/code
notable_findings.evidence: docs/AFI_PLUGINS_OVERVIEW.md:5 'canonical extension surface for afi-reactor (DAG orchestrator) and afi-core (ElizaOS runtime layer).' ; src/types/plugin.ts:23 '| "afi-reactor" // Runs as DAG node in afi-reactor'
notable_findings.recommendation: Add a one-line note that afi-reactor is the REFERENCE DAG runtime and that the runtimeTarget enum is extensible for other conformant orchestrators; avoids presenting reactor as protocol law.

notable_findings.title: configSchemaRef / inputSchemaRef are unvalidated string labels, no schema linkage
notable_findings.evidence: src/registry/pluginRegistry.ts:43 'configSchemaRef: "BlofinTrendPullbackConfig"' ; src/types/plugin.ts:76-77 'Reference to config schema (JSON Schema URL or Zod schema name)'; no resolver/validator exists.
notable_findings.recommendation: Bind these refs to the normative plugin-manifest/config schemas in afi-config and implement the planned validate:plugins so registry entries are provably conformant.

notable_findings.title: AGENTS.md references src/examples/ directory that does not exist
notable_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:68 'src/examples/ - Example plugin implementations' and :122 'Add example plugins in src/examples/'; no examples/ dir present (find shows only types/registry/templates/tests).
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale: remove or create src/examples/; reconcile AGENTS.md directory list with actual tree.

notable_findings.title: PoInsight / PoI scoring math described but only as TODO weighted-average placeholder
notable_findings.evidence: src/templates/scorer/signalScorerTemplate.ts:114 '6. Compute composite score (weighted average: a*PoI + b*PoInsight)' ; scorer/README.md:32 'Composite score (weighted average: a*PoI + b*PoInsight)'. No public-vs-proprietary surface split is modeled.
notable_findings.recommendation: When implementing, locate deterministic scoring + public/proprietary surface split in the reactor/scoring spec; this repo should reference, not redefine, the canonical scoring contract.
